description_long: This object is a vibrant red flower with multiple broad, overlapping petals radiating outward from a central core. The petals are slightly ruffled and have a naturalistic, velvety texture, with several white-tipped stamens protruding from the center. The flower is attached to a thick, green stem that appears sturdy and slightly textured, with a subtle sheen. There are no visible leaves attached to the stem, and the flower head is large in proportion to the stem. The coloration is predominantly a deep, saturated red with hints of orange, and the stem is a muted green. The overall appearance is reminiscent of an Amaryllis or similar ornamental flower commonly found in urban parks or gardens.
description: A large, red, multi-petaled flower with a thick green stem and prominent white-tipped stamens. The petals are broad, overlapping, and slightly ruffled, giving the flower a lush, ornamental look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full face of the flower, with petals radiating outward and stamens clearly visible in the center.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the layered arrangement of the petals and reveals more of the stem's length and texture.
description_view_2: A slightly angled view showing the depth of the flower head and the overlapping nature of the petals.
description_view_3: Another side view, emphasizing the open cup shape of the flower and the curvature of the petals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with multiple layers, overlapping each other symmetrically around the central axis. The arrangement is typical of many ornamental flowers, with a clear radial symmetry and some petals partially covering others.
